2006-06-01.title: Prince Albert of Monaco Admits 2nd Child
2006-06-01.abstract: Prince Albert of Monaco acknowledged that he is the father of a Jazmin Grace Rotolo, a 14-year-old American girl.

2006-06-01.title: Quake Upsets the Cradle of Indonesian Culture
2006-06-01.abstract: The region affected by the quake is part of the ancient kingdom of Yogyakarta, making it a popular destination.

2006-06-01.title: In Paris Suburbs, Worrying Attack by Youths
2006-06-01.abstract: The youths threw rocks at police and set a dozen cars ablaze in a second night of violence reminiscent of last fall's unrest.

2006-06-01.title: Britain: Game Over, and Here Are the Keys
2006-06-01.abstract: After a fierce public debate, Deputy Prime Minister John Prescott said he would no longer use an official country residence where he was photographed playing croquet while technically running the government. Acknowledging a personal, political setback, Mr. Prescott said his continued use of the state-financed mansion called Dorneywood, on 214 acres of parkland,

2006-06-01.title: Zimbabwe: A $100,000 Bill Worth a Buck, if That
2006-06-01.abstract: Struggling to cope with a 1,000-plus percent annual inflation rate, Zimbabwe introduced a new $100,000 bank note, double the $50,000 note that had been the largest denomination in circulation. Still, the new note is only worth about one American dollar at official exchange rates, and draws only about 30 American cents on the black market, which more accurately reflects its value. The new note may help ease a growing currency shortage. The United Nations regional news agency quoted Zimbabwe Reserve Bank officials as saying that the country had run short of money after the government ran out of the foreign exchange needed to buy the imported ink and paper required to print bills.

2006-06-02.title: British Lecturers Protest Over Pay
2006-06-02.abstract: After months of failed negotiations, hundreds of British college lecturers are protesting for higher pay, which may prohibit seniors from graduating.

2006-06-02.title: Dispute on South Ossetia Raises Tensions With Georgia
2006-06-02.abstract: Russia rejected demands by Georgia to obtain entry visas for its troops entering Georgia's breakaway republic.

2006-06-02.title: It's Springtime for Soccer, and for Rowdy England Fans
2006-06-02.abstract: As England's soccer fans prepare to visit Germany for the World Cup, another item has been added to their long "verboten" list: Don't mention the war.

2006-06-02.title: Iraqi Assails U.S. for Strikes on Civilians
2006-06-02.abstract: The prime minister of Iraq said that U.S. troops crush civilians "with their vehicles and kill them just on suspicion."

2006-06-02.title: Eight Oil Workers Abducted From Rig Off Nigeria
2006-06-02.abstract: The kidnapping was the latest of a string of violent incidents meant to disrupt oil production in the country's oil-rich Niger Delta region.

2006-06-03.title: Ivory Coast: U.N. Force Expands
2006-06-03.abstract: The Security Council voted to add 1,500 peacekeepers to the 7,000 already in Ivory Coast as temporary help in a political transition to elections in October. A failed coup attempt in 2002 left the government controlling the south and rebels holding the north. Secretary General Kofi Annan had asked for 3,900 new troops and police officers, but the United States, which pays 27 percent of the peacekeeping budget, protested.

2006-06-03.title: Man Shot in Counterterrorism Raid in London
2006-06-03.abstract: British police officers shot, wounded and arrested a 23-year-old man in a raid on an east London home before dawn on Friday.

2006-06-03.title: U.N. Strengthens Call for a Global Battle Against AIDS
2006-06-03.abstract: The General Assembly adopted a strongly worded declaration that presses the world's  nations to step up the battle against AIDS.

2006-06-03.title: Peru's Voters May Turn to a Tested, and Failed, Leader
2006-06-03.abstract: Alan García, the front-runner going into the runoff election on Sunday, is running against his own record as president in the 1980's.

2006-06-03.title: Pakistan: World Bank to Step Up Loans
2006-06-03.abstract: The World Bank's new strategy for Pakistan forecasts doubling lending to the country over the next four years to as much as $6.5 billion. Up to $1 billion would help with recovery from the October 2005 earthquake. Much of the balance would finance projects in health, education, roads, railways, electricity and water. The bank praised Pakistan for policies that helped increase economic growth to 8.4 percent in 2004 and 2005, from 3.3 percent between 1997 and 2002. It said the government, bolstered by foreign aid, had exceeded targets for spending on health, education and antipoverty programs.

2006-06-04.title: A Talk at Lunch That Shifted the Stance on Iran
2006-06-04.abstract: A discussion between Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice and President Bush two months ago led to an effort to devise a different policy toward Iran.

2006-06-04.title: Gunmen Kill Russian Embassy Worker in Iraq
2006-06-04.abstract: Gunmen ambushed five Russian Embassy workers on Saturday in Baghdad, killing one and kidnapping the others.

2006-06-04.title: War's Risks Include Toll on Training Values
2006-06-04.abstract: In counterinsurgency warfare, a failure to distinguish civilians from the enemy has bedeviled most powerful armies fighting a shadow enemy in an unfamiliar country.

2006-06-04.title: Race to Lead Japan May Turn on Asia Ties
2006-06-04.abstract: Japan's relations with China and South Korea have chilled because of several disputes over history and territory.

2006-06-04.title: Spanish Judge Calls for Closing U.S. Prison at Guantánamo
2006-06-04.abstract: Criticism in Europe reflects both concern among Europeans that the United States has abandoned its core values and a widespread anti-Americanism among Europe's Muslim population.

2006-06-05.title: Ex-President Wins in Peru in Stunning Comeback
2006-06-05.abstract: Alan García was elected again, 16 years after his first presidency ended in economic collapse and violence.

2006-06-05.title: Quake Adds to Fear That Indonesian Volcano Will Erupt
2006-06-05.abstract: Fears of Mount Merapi erupting increased significantly after the earthquake that struck the nearby Yogyakarta area on May 27.

2006-06-05.title: Gunmen Kill 20 Passengers at Fake Checkpoint in Iraq
2006-06-05.abstract: Gunmen set up a fake checkpoint, stopped two minibuses and executed the passengers in a killing brutal even by Iraq's standards.

2006-06-05.title: An Upbeat Rumsfeld and Vietnam Agree to Broaden Ties
2006-06-05.abstract: During the defense secretary's visit to Hanoi, the two nations had decided to step up "exchanges at all levels of the military."

2006-06-05.title: Canadian Authorities Expect More Arrests
2006-06-05.abstract: The arrests over the weekend were tied to a broad terrorist plot to make bombs.

2006-06-06.title: In Indonesia, Rumsfeld Is Warned on U.S. Image
2006-06-06.abstract: Indonesia's defense minister warned the Bush administration today that its approach to fighting terrorism was alienating Muslims.

2006-06-06.title: Minor Figure in Iraqi Kidnapping Gets a Life Sentence
2006-06-06.abstract: The kidnapping of Margaret Hassan, a British-Iraqi aid worker, opened a more dangerous era for foreign nationals working in Iraq.

2006-06-06.title: Iraq to Free Detainees in Bid for Reconciliation
2006-06-06.abstract: Iraq's new government said that it would release 2,500 detainees and that it would adopt a "national reconciliation" plan.

2006-06-06.title: Rumsfeld, Visiting Vietnam, Seals Accord to Deepen Military Cooperation
2006-06-06.abstract: The U.S. and Vietnam agreed Monday to increase their military contacts and discuss ways to broaden their defense cooperation.

2006-06-06.title: Putin, Alongside Kissinger, Praises Ties with U.S.
2006-06-06.abstract: The Russian leader hailed a recent six-nation agreement on Iran as a sign that their two nations can still work well together.

2006-06-07.title: Report Cites Europe's Role in C.I.A. Renditions
2006-06-07.abstract: REPORT CITES EUROPE'S ROLE IN C.I.A. RENDITIONS.

2006-06-07.title: Canada Saw Plot to Seize Officials
2006-06-07.abstract: The plot involved a series of violent attacks and the beheading of Canada's prime minister, prosecutors said.

2006-06-07.title: Indonesian Scolds U.S. on Terrorism Fight
2006-06-07.abstract: Indonesia's defense minister warned the Bush administration Tuesday that its approach to fighting terrorism is alienating Muslims.

2006-06-07.title: China Postpones Trial Date for Times Researcher
2006-06-07.abstract: Authorities have postponed an informal trial date for Zhao Yan, accused of fraud and disclosing state secrets, his lawyer said today.

2006-06-07.title: Afghans Say 52 Admit Violence After U.S. Truck Crash
2006-06-07.abstract: Afghan officials announced they had detained 250 people, 52 of whom had admitted involvement in the violence and looting.

2006-06-08.title: Afghans Raise Toll of Dead From May Riots in Kabul to 17
2006-06-08.abstract: Afghan officials raised the death toll from the May 29 riots to 17 and said 140 people had been accused of involvement in the rioting.

2006-06-08.title: After the Divorce, Serbia Alone Mourns the Lost Partnership
2006-06-08.abstract: While Montenegro has been triumphant in asserting its new independence, in Serbia there is a sense of diminishment and loss.

2006-06-08.title: China Postpones Times Researcher's Trial
2006-06-08.abstract: Chinese authorities have postponed the trial date for Times researcher Zhao Yan, accused of fraud and disclosing state secrets.

2006-06-08.title: Efforts by C.I.A. Fail in Somalia, Officials Charge
2006-06-08.abstract: A covert C.I.A. effort to finance Somali warlords has empowered the Islamic groups it was intended to marginalize, critics say.

2006-06-08.title: Hunt Ends With Pair of 500-Lb. Bombs
2006-06-08.abstract: The American military finally hunted down and killed Abu Musab al-Zarqawi by homing in on the activities of the terrorist leader's "spiritual adviser."

2006-06-09.title: Some Rich Nations Slow With Pledged Aid
2006-06-09.abstract: Promises from leaders of some of the world's richest nations to significantly increase aid to combat global poverty by 2010, have thus far fallen short.

2006-06-09.title: Sri Lanka: Rebels Reject European Monitors and Skip Talks
2006-06-09.abstract: Tamil Tiger rebels refused to meet with a delegation of the Sri Lankan government in talks in Oslo facilitated by Europeans. The government said it had been told that the presence of representatives of Denmark, Finland and Sweden, members of the Nordic cease-fire mission, was

2006-06-09.title: Zarqawi Said to Briefly Survive Airstrike
2006-06-09.abstract: The military said he turned away when U.S. troops approached the stretcher on which Iraqi police officers had placed him.

2006-06-09.title: China Bans 'Code' After Warning From Catholics
2006-06-09.abstract: Small protests were cited as evidence that the film was becoming a political risk.

2006-06-09.title: Troop Cuts in Iraq Won't Meet Goal This Year, Officials Say
2006-06-09.abstract: There is little chance the United States can reach the milestone of reducing American troop levels in Iraq to 100,000 by December.

2006-06-10.title: For Anniversary, Thai King Gets More Praise
2006-06-10.abstract: For a moment on Friday it seemed that Thailand's king really might be divine, as all around the country his subjects paused to celebrate his 60th year on the throne.

2006-06-10.title: 7 Are Slain in Ingushetia
2006-06-10.abstract: Two pro-Kremlin officials were assassinated minutes apart on Friday in Russia's volatile North Caucasus.

2006-06-10.title: Clan Calls Death of 'Martyr' a Blessing
2006-06-10.abstract: In the town of Zarqa, Abu Musab al-Zarqawi was the local hero who went off into the world and made his family and neighbors proud.

2006-06-10.title: China Cancels 'Da Vinci' Movie
2006-06-10.abstract: The move was a concession to Chinese Catholic groups that warned that the film threatened social stability.

2006-06-10.title: Somalia: U.S. Calls  International Meeting After Gains by Islamists
2006-06-10.abstract: The Bush administration will convene an international meeting in New York next week to discuss strategy on Somalia, where secular warlords supported by the United States appear to have been ousted by Islamist militias. The meeting, announced by the State Department, comes as the administration is under fire from critics who say the Central Intelligence Agency's covert campaign to finance the warlords may have thwarted counterterrorism efforts inside Somalia and empowered the same Islamic groups it was intended to marginalize. Officials from the United Nations, Europe and Africa are expected to take part in a meeting of the Somalia "contact group," which is intended "to promote concerted action and coordination to support the Somalia transitional federal institutions," said Sean McCormack, the State Department spokesman.

2006-06-11.title: Lebanese Leader's Killing Probably a Suicide Bombing, U.N. Finds
2006-06-11.abstract: The head of an inquiry said that he was making "considerable progress," but he revealed nothing new about who was behind the assassination of Rafik Hariri.

2006-06-11.title: Hamas Fires Rockets Into Israel, Ending 16-Month Truce
2006-06-11.abstract: Hamas fired the rockets after seven Palestinians were killed on a Gaza beach by an errant Israeli shell.

2006-06-11.title: U.S. Seeking New Strategy for Buttressing Iraq's Government
2006-06-11.abstract: President Bush on Monday will begin a strategy session on Iraq that one official called a "last, best chance to get this right."

2006-06-11.title: Mob Takes Car From U.S. Patrol in Ontario
2006-06-11.abstract: American Indian protesters tried to run over a police officer using a United States Border Patrol car, police said.

2006-06-11.title: Terrorists Trained by Zarqawi Went Abroad, Jordan Says
2006-06-11.abstract: At the time of his death, Abu Musab al-Zarqawi was still trying to transform his organization into a global terrorist operation.

2006-06-12.title: China Posts Record Trade Surplus of $13 Billion
2006-06-12.abstract: The record raised the prospect of renewed trade frictions with the United States and Europe.

2006-06-12.title: Mexico's Enforcers Take On Election-Year Mudslingers
2006-06-12.abstract: Courts have given Mexico's Federal Election Institute the authority to ban any speech that besmirches a candidate's reputation.

2006-06-12.title: Errant Shell Turns Girl Into Palestinian Icon
2006-06-12.abstract: A sixth-grade student whose image was televised around the world as she knelt by her dead family on a Gaza beach unwittingly became a symbol of Palestinian pain.

2006-06-12.title: Violence Grows in Palestinian Dispute
2006-06-12.abstract: Security personnel of the Fatah faction went on a rampage tonight in Ramallah after Hamas gunmen attacked a Fatah building in Gaza.

2006-06-12.title: Russia Bargains for Bigger Stake in West's Energy
2006-06-12.abstract: Russia is seeking greater latitude to invest in utilities, pipelines and other infrastructure in the U.S. and Europe.

2006-06-13.title: Attacks Kill Dozens of Iraqis in Kirkuk
2006-06-13.abstract: The seven bombings today represented an attack of unusual coordination that officials suggested may have been retaliation for the arrest of a terrorist cell.

2006-06-13.title: Violence Erupts Between Fatah and Hamas
2006-06-13.abstract: Security personnel of the Fatah faction went on a rampage after Hamas gunmen attacked the headquarters of the Fatah-dominated security forces.

2006-06-13.title: Trip Was Kept Secret From Key Cabinet Members
2006-06-13.abstract: The planning for the president's visit was kept so quiet that several of his highest-level aides learned about it only this morning.

2006-06-13.title: World's Image of U.S. Slips Further, Survey Shows
2006-06-13.abstract: Favorable views of the U.S. dropped sharply over the past year, according to a survey by the Pew Research Center.

2006-06-13.title: British Police Criticized for Raid on Muslim
2006-06-13.abstract: A Muslim man who was shot during a raid said police did not identify themselves and gave no warning before opening fire and beating him.

2006-06-14.title: Global Image of the U.S. Is Worsening, Survey Finds
2006-06-14.abstract: Favorable views of the U.S. dropped sharply over the past year, according to a survey by the Pew Research Center.

2006-06-14.title: Bush Makes Surprise Visit to Iraq to Press Leadership
2006-06-14.abstract: President Bush said that the time had come for Iraqis to pull together to save the faltering American-sponsored democracy in Iraq.

2006-06-14.title: China Tightens Control With State Secrets Law
2006-06-14.abstract: The upcoming trial of a New York Times researcher is evidence of China's efforts to contain public debate.

2006-06-14.title: Economic Forum Opens as National Stocks Plunge
2006-06-14.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin opened a conference billed as the Russian Davos on the same day that the stock market suffered its biggest one-day drop since Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky was arrested in 2003.

2006-06-14.title: Afghans Call for Release of 47 From Guantánamo
2006-06-14.abstract: An Afghan government delegation said that half of the Afghans still being held were not guilty of serious crimes.

2006-06-15.title: Militant Farmer to Run for President
2006-06-15.abstract: José Bové, the farmer who has attracted worldwide attention for his protests against globalization, said he would run in the presidential election next year, pledging to pull left-wing votes from the Socialists, the main opposition party.

2006-06-15.title: Man Quits Job and Sends Tarantula
2006-06-15.abstract: Mahlon Hector, 22, pleaded guilty to sending a live tarantula in a box to a female co-worker at a Marks & Spencer store in Leicester. At the same time, he handed his bosses there his resignation. Neither the intended recipient nor the spider was hurt. Mr. Hector, 22, is to be sentenced later on a charge of sending a threat. He did not reveal his motive in court. When workers at the store discovered the Mexican red-kneed tarantula — considered venomous but docile and commonly used in films because of its scary appearance — they alerted the Royal Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, which took it away.

2006-06-15.title: 2 Trapped in Bunker After Volcano's Eruption
2006-06-15.abstract: Two people escaped to a bunker which became engulfed by lava and volcanic debris on the southern slopes of Mount Merapi.

2006-06-15.title: Arrest of Business Owner Resurrects the Furniture Affair'
2006-06-15.abstract: Almost invariably, front pages in Russia on Thursday reported the arrest of the former owner of the country's largest furniture stores, on charges of tax evasion and smuggling.

2006-06-15.title: Iran Quietly Learns of Penalties in a Nuclear Incentives Deal
2006-06-15.abstract: When a formal incentives package was presented in writing in Tehran last week, it omitted a long list of potential punishments should Iran reject it.

2006-06-16.title: 8 Afghans Killed on Way to Work at U.S. Air Base
2006-06-16.abstract: A bomb exploded on a bus taking workers to the United States military air base at Kandahar in southern Afghanistan on Thursday.

2006-06-16.title: Japan: Parliament Passes Law to Combat Suicide
2006-06-16.abstract: Parliament enacted a law requiring the government and employers to combat the soaring suicide rate, which is among the highest in the world. The law mandates that central and local governments come up with policies to prevent suicides, requires the government to report annually on its progress and asks employers to take better care of workers' mental health. A total of 32,552 Japanese took their own lives in 2005.

2006-06-16.title: Canada: Agents Asked Terror Suspects' Parents for Help
2006-06-16.abstract: Months before arresting 17 suspects in an alleged terrorism plot this month, agents of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service warned some of the suspects' parents that their sons were adopting "extremist ideology," The Globe and Mail reported. The accused, mostly in their late teens and early 20's, face charges of participating in a plot to attack Canadian landmarks and behead the prime minister. The approach taken by the agents to speak to the suspects' parents was in line with a strategy to scare off the accused by not hiding the fact that they were being monitored. "C.S.I.S. officers had approached some parents to let them know that C.S.I.S. suspected their children were becoming adherents to an extremist ideology," a spokeswoman for the service, Barb Campion, told the newspaper.

2006-06-16.title: Jailed Times Researcher Gets One-Day Trial in China
2006-06-16.abstract: The closed trial of a New York Times researcher accused of fraud and disclosing state secrets ended here today without the court reaching a verdict.

2006-06-16.title: Summit Ends With Apparent Triumph for Putin in Tussles Over Iran
2006-06-16.abstract: A summit marking the five-year anniversary of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization ended with an apparent triumph for Russia and President Vladimir V. Putin in the diplomatic tussles over Iran's nuclear program.

2006-06-17.title: Mont-St.-Michel to Be an Island Again
2006-06-17.abstract: With the start this week of a $190 million earth-moving project aimed at reversing centuries of silting, the medieval abbey of Mont-St.-Michel, a jewel in France's architectural crown, will become an island once again. After Paris, Mont-St.-Michel, built 1,300 years ago on a craggy rock off the northwestern Normandy coast, is France's main tourist attraction, but the encroaching spread of surrounding mud flats has spoiled the island-like character of the Benedictine abbey, now cut off from the mainland only at very high tides.

2006-06-17.title: Wes Hill, 76, Cautious Friend of Niagara Torrents and Eddies, Dies
2006-06-17.abstract: Wes Hill mastered the currents and crevices of the Niagara River and its falls and became an expert at saving lives and recovering bodies.

2006-06-17.title: House Rejects Timetable for Withdrawal From Iraq
2006-06-17.abstract: The vote, which followed a day of intense, often angry debate, reflected sharp partisan divisions over the war.

2006-06-17.title: U.S. Agrees to Plan for Stipends for Some Palestinians
2006-06-17.abstract: Funds will be transferred through the World Bank to poor Palestinians, some of whom had previously drawn salaries from the Palestinian government.

2006-06-17.title: Contradictions Cloud Inquiry Into 24 Iraqi Deaths
2006-06-17.abstract: How and why a deadly attack in Haditha, Iraq, occurred and who ultimately bears responsibility are matters of profound dispute.

2006-06-18.title: Russian Troops Kill Leader of Chechen Separatists
2006-06-18.abstract: Russian special forces killed the leader of Chechnya's separatists, dealing a blow to a rebel movement that has carried out terrorist attacks but recently has appeared to be seriously weakened.

2006-06-18.title: Somali Islamists Cement Control of South Amid Border Tension
2006-06-18.abstract: The leader of the Islamists who now control most of southern Somalia accused the United States of orchestrating what he called a border incursion by hundreds of Ethiopian troops.

2006-06-18.title: U.S. Widens Search for 2 Soldiers Missing in Iraq
2006-06-18.abstract: The expanded search effort came as U.S. troops made a new push to break the insurgents' grip on Ramadi.

2006-06-18.title: 4 Months Into Aid Cutoff, Gazans Barely Scrape By
2006-06-18.abstract: Palestinians in Gaza are struggling to cope  without salaries from the Hamas-led Palestinian Authority.

2006-06-18.title: At the Heart of the United Front on Iran, Vagueness on Crucial Terms
2006-06-18.abstract: The demand for Tehran to stop all activities related to the enrichment of uranium seems precise, but what it actually means is unclear.

2006-06-19.title: Search for G.I.'s Expands; Troops Surround Ramadi
2006-06-19.abstract: The search for two missing U.S. soldiers intensified as U.S. troops made a new push to break the grip insurgents hold on Ramadi.

2006-06-19.title: In Kazakhstan, Putin Wins One and Loses One
2006-06-19.abstract: The presidents of Russia and Kazakhstan watched the world's first Kazakh satellite soar into orbit with the help of a Russian-built rocket.

2006-06-19.title: In Somalia, Islamic Militias Fight Culture Wars
2006-06-19.abstract: Flush from a military victory, Islamic militiamen have begun waging cultural battles in and around Mogadishu.

2006-06-19.title: European Meets Israeli and Abbas on Palestinian Aid Plan
2006-06-19.abstract: A top European Union official today pushed forward a new plan aimed at easing the increasingly serious financial crunch among Palestinians.

2006-06-19.title: 3 U.S. Soldiers Accused of Murder and Death Threat
2006-06-19.abstract: Three soldiers are charged with threatening a comrade with death if he cooperated with an investigation of their roles in the deaths of three Iraqi detainees.

2006-06-20.title: Army Cancels Contract for Iraqi Prison
2006-06-20.abstract: The cancellation of the $99.1 million contract is another harsh rebuke for Parsons, one of the largest companies working in Iraq.

2006-06-20.title: Pigeons May Coo but They Aren't Cute, the Mayor Says
2006-06-20.abstract: Mayor Ken Livingstone has made it his mission since he took office to eradicate pigeons from Trafalgar Square, long the center of pigeon activity in London.

2006-06-20.title: Japan to Pull Its Ground Troops Out of Iraq
2006-06-20.abstract: The decision ends the country's most significant overseas deployment since World War II.

2006-06-20.title: Putin Nominee for Prosecutor Draws Praise From All Sides
2006-06-20.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin named Justice Minister Yuri Chaika as his candidate for chief prosecutor Monday, an announcement covered on the front page of all leading newspapers.

2006-06-20.title: Murder Charges for 3 G.I.'s in Iraq
2006-06-20.abstract: The three soldiers are suspected of killing three detainees and threatening a soldier with death if he reported the shootings.

2006-06-21.title: Former Liberian President in The Hague for Trial
2006-06-21.abstract: The long list of atrocities attributed to Charles G. Taylor includes running a rebel force that killed tens of thousands of civilians.

2006-06-21.title: Pentagon Picks Troops for Iraq, Maintaining Current Contingent
2006-06-21.abstract: The announcement represents a decision that for planning purposes at least, U.S. troops in Iraq would be replaced on a one-for-one basis for now.

2006-06-21.title: U.S. Says 2 Bodies Retrieved in Iraq Were Brutalized
2006-06-21.abstract: An American military official said the bodies showed evidence of "severe trauma" and that they could not be positively identified.

2006-06-21.title: French Premier, on Assembly Floor, Rails at Socialist's 'Cowardice'
2006-06-21.abstract: When the Socialist Party leader berated the government for its handling of the crisis at Airbus, the prime minister lost control.

2006-06-21.title: A Campus for 'Scholars, Not Fighters' on a Settlement Site
2006-06-21.abstract: A Palestinian college is growing from the rubble of an uprooted Israeli settlement.

2006-06-22.title: Uprising in Uzbekistan
2006-06-22.abstract: C.J. Chivers examines newly available videotape of the May 2005 uprising in Uzbekistan. (Producer: Adam B. Ellick)

2006-06-22.title: Botched Israeli Strike Kills Palestinian in Gaza
2006-06-22.abstract: It was the latest in a series of botched air strikes that have killed a dozen Palestinian civilians in the last eight days.

2006-06-22.title: Bush Compares Iraq War to Hungary's Uprising
2006-06-22.abstract: A hillside speech in Budapest concluded a two-day European trip aimed at promoting trans-Atlantic unity.

2006-06-22.title: Awaiting 2 Soldiers' ID's, Learning to Live With Grief
2006-06-22.abstract: For those who knew the two, the reality of what have been reported as brutal deaths seemed to be setting in on Wednesday.

2006-06-22.title: Hussein Lawyer Seized and Slain in Baghdad Raid
2006-06-22.abstract: The defense lawyer was the 10th person associated with the court trying Saddam Hussein to be killed in the last 18 months.

2006-06-23.title: Coalition Emerges in Ukraine, But So Do the Tensions
2006-06-23.abstract: The announcement on Thursday of the reformation of an Orange Coalition in the Ukrainian parliament, after nearly three months of negotiations, was widely discussed throughout the Russian press.

2006-06-23.title: Montenegro: Security Council Backs U.N. Bid
2006-06-23.abstract: The United Nations Security Council recommended to the General Assembly that Montenegro be admitted as the 192nd member of the United Nations. The Assembly is expected to approve membership next week. Montenegro formally declared independence from its union with Serbia on June 3, after a referendum. Under the union's Constitution, Serbia inherited membership in the United Nations.

2006-06-23.title: Japan: Sex, It's Just What the Doctor Ordered.
2006-06-23.abstract: "Japanese people simply aren't having sex," Dr. Kunio Kitamura, a gynecologist and the director of the Japan Family Planning Association, told The Japan Times, adding that more sex is needed to reverse Japan's plummeting birthrate. He spoke after an association survey of 936 people between the ages of 16 and 49 showed that 31 percent had not had sex for more than a month "for no particular reason." Last year, Japan's fertility rate — the average number of children a woman bears in her lifetime — fell to an all-time low of 1.25. Demographers say a rate of 2.1 is needed to keep a population from declining.

2006-06-23.title: Bird Flu Passed From Son to Father, W.H.O. Says
2006-06-23.abstract: Investigators today confirmed the first case of human-to-human transmission of the disease.

2006-06-23.title: Iraqi Government Declares State of Emergency
2006-06-23.abstract: The state of emergency imposed today followed a gunfight involving members of the Mahdi Army militia.

2006-06-24.title: Tortoise Dies at 176
2006-06-24.abstract: Harriet, a Galápagos land tortoise that held the Guinness World Record as the world's oldest living animal in captivity, died at Australia Zoo in southeast Queensland at the age of 176. DNA testing vouched for both her age and her birth in the Galápagos Islands. Mistaken for a male tortoise and called Harry for more than a century, she produced no offspring.

2006-06-24.title: Cheney Assails Press on Report on Bank Data
2006-06-24.abstract: Vice President Dick Cheney harshly criticized the news media for disclosing an operation he said was legal and "absolutely essential" to fighting terrorism.

2006-06-24.title: East Timor: President Vows to Stay On
2006-06-24.abstract: President Xanana Gusmão appeared to withdraw a threat to resign, but the prime minister, Mari Alkatiri, was reported to be about to quit.

2006-06-24.title: Fear Invades a Once-Comfortable Iraqi Enclave
2006-06-24.abstract: Residents of a Baghdad neighborhood worry that a wave of violence will begin to encroach on them.

2006-06-24.title: Mystery Deepens on Possible Avian Flu Case in China in 2003
2006-06-24.abstract: Did China have a death from avian flu two years before it admitted having any human cases?

2006-06-25.title: U.S. General in Iraq Outlines Troop Cuts
2006-06-25.abstract: His plan projects sharp reductions in the U.S. military presence in Iraq by the end of 2007, with the first cuts coming this September.

2006-06-25.title: Group Hopes to Prove Chemicals Killed Kurds
2006-06-25.abstract: Experts who have studied Iraq's use of chemical weapons are attempting to define the scope of a distant tragedy.

2006-06-25.title: Wary of U.S., Syria and Iran Strengthen Ties
2006-06-25.abstract: The two governments are tightening relations on several fronts as power shifts away from the Sunni to Shiites.

2006-06-25.title: 4 U.S. Soldiers Die; Cleric Is Freed After Protests
2006-06-25.abstract: The four deaths brought the number of American soldiers killed in Iraq this month to 46. None of the four were identified.

2006-06-25.title: Hussein Thinks He Will Get Death Penalty but Sees Escape Hatch, His Lawyer Says
2006-06-25.abstract: Saddam Hussein believes he will be asked by the U.S. to help tamp down Iraq's insurgency, and might again become President.

2006-06-26.title: Militants' Raid on Israel Raises Gaza Tension
2006-06-26.abstract: Israel threatened a harsh response to a raid by Palestinian militants in which two soldiers were killed and one kidnapped.

2006-06-26.title: Amid Iraqi Chaos, Schools Fill After Long Decline
2006-06-26.abstract: Enrollment in Iraqi schools has risen every year since the U.S. invasion, reversing more than a decade of declines.

2006-06-26.title: British Envoy Urges 'Early Response' From Iran's Leader
2006-06-26.abstract: The new British ambassador in Tehran pressed Iran for a response to the proposal aimed at ending a standoff over Iran's nuclear program.

2006-06-26.title: New Militant Leader Emerges in Mogadishu
2006-06-26.abstract: The rise of a Muslim sheik whom the U.S. has linked to terrorism is seen as a setback for Bush policy in Somalia.

2006-06-26.title: China Weighs Fines for Reports on 'Sudden Events'
2006-06-26.abstract: The law would be a powerful new tool to restrict coverage of events that the authorities prefer to keep secret.

2006-06-27.title: Iran's Leader Sends Mixed Signals on Nuclear Talks
2006-06-27.abstract: The White House spokesman, Tony Snow, called the remarks by Ayatollah Ali Khamenei "ambiguous."

2006-06-27.title: China Covers Up Violent Suppression of Village Protest
2006-06-27.abstract: Chinese authorities have said little or nothing about a police shooting on demonstrators last December and killed as many as 30 people.

2006-06-27.title: Group Tries to Block Program Giving Data to U.S.
2006-06-27.abstract: Privacy advocates hope to stop a banking consortium from providing the U.S. with secret data to track terrorists.

2006-06-27.title: Israeli Troops Move Into Gaza; Bridges Are Hit
2006-06-27.abstract: Israeli troops entered Gaza and its planes attacked three bridges and a power station tonight in an operation to rescue a soldier abducted by militants. Earlier, Palestinian political factions completed a draft agreement aimed at a national unity government. At right, Israeli soldiers preparing to cross into Gaza.

2006-06-27.title: Debate on Russia and Iraq Follows Killing of 4 Diplomats
2006-06-27.abstract: "By all appearances, the irrevocable has happened," the Russian Foreign Ministry said Monday, announcing the death of four Russian diplomats who were taken hostage earlier this month in Iraq.

2006-06-28.title: Iraqi Led Bombing of Shiite Shrine, Official Says
2006-06-28.abstract: The bombing that set off waves of sectarian killing was allegedly the brainchild of an Iraqi member of Al Qaeda.

2006-06-28.title: Putin Puts Out 'Kill' Order on Iraqi Kidnappers
2006-06-28.abstract: The order reflected the deep shock and anger that has unfolded since the killing of five Russians in Iraq.

2006-06-28.title: Tomb Yields Many Mysteries, but no Mummy
2006-06-28.abstract: Egypt's chief Egyptologist had a theory that a 3,300-year-old tomb was the burial place of King Tut's mother, Queen Kiya.

2006-06-28.title: Senator Says North Korean Missile Firing May Not Be Imminent
2006-06-28.abstract: While North Korea showed clear signs of preparing for a test, it was not clear if the launch was imminent, Senator John W. Warner said.

2006-06-28.title: Israel Threatens to Widen Conflict Over Captured Soldier
2006-06-28.abstract: Israeli tanks hunkered down inside Gaza today and warplanes flew over the house of Syrian president Bashar Assad.

2006-06-29.title: Behind Bush's Fury, a Vow Made in 2001
2006-06-29.abstract: The bank program disclosure puts publicity-shy bankers in the spotlight and suggests that the U.S. can't keep secrets.

2006-06-29.title: Sectarian Fighting Breaks Out North of Baghdad
2006-06-29.abstract: Intense clashes erupted today between Shiite and Sunni Arab fighters in a village north of Baghdad.

2006-06-29.title: Israel Admits the Gloves Are Off in Dealing With Hamas
2006-06-29.abstract: Israel's arrest early today of some 64 members of Hamas marks a significant change in Israel's policy toward the Hamas government.

2006-06-29.title: Bush's Farewell to Koizumi Is Also a Reward
2006-06-29.abstract: President Bush is giving Japan's prime minister, a huge Elvis Presley fan, a private presidential tour of Graceland.

2006-06-29.title: France: Prime Minister Has 'No Presidential Ambitions'
2006-06-29.abstract: Prime Minister Dominique de Villepin, the target of fierce criticism in recent months, said he had no plans to be a candidate in the presidential election next April.

2006-06-30.title: Serbia: Court Finds Milosevic Behind Rival's Murder
Diplomats Push Iran to Reply Soon to Incentives Offer
2006-06-30.abstract: Iran should respond by July 5 to a proposal meant to resolve the standoff over its nuclear program.

2006-06-30.title: Congo: Campaigning Begins
2006-06-30.abstract: Campaigning for presidential and parliamentary elections on July 30 — the first free vote in more than 40 years — officially began. There are 33 presidential and nearly 10,000 parliamentary candidates. The popular opposition party U.D.P.S. is boycotting the vote and wants supporters to block or remove any sign of campaigning.

2006-06-30.title: Foreign Policy Tries a Little Shake, Rattle and Roll
2006-06-30.abstract: Graceland joined Yalta in the annals of U.S. history with a visit today by President Bush and Japan's leader.

2006-06-30.title: Dispute Over Minister Topples Dutch Government
2006-06-30.abstract: The center-right government collapsed after a coalition party failed to force the country's controversial immigration minister to resign.
